pattern: Binary-search the answer space from 1 to max(nums); for each candidate divisor compute the ceiling-division sum in O(n) and narrow toward the smallest value that keeps the sum at or below the threshold.
hints: The ceiling sum is monotonically non-increasing as the divisor grows, so binary search works: when the sum is above threshold you need a larger divisor, otherwise record the candidate and try smaller.
Ceiling division of a by d is (a + d - 1) // d, or equivalently -(-a // d) in Python.
Binary-search lo=1, hi=max(nums). Compute the sum with mid as divisor; if sum <= threshold, mid is a valid answer, set hi=mid, else lo=mid+1.

edge_inputs.description: single element; divisor equals the element
threshold equals array length; every ceiling is 1 so divisor is max(nums)
threshold is very large; divisor 1 already satisfies
threshold equals sum when divisor is 1 so divisor 1 is the answer

stress.description: large random array with threshold ensuring an answer exists
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000) for _ in range(n)]
    # threshold in [n, sum(nums)] guarantees an answer exists
    threshold = rng.randint(n, sum(nums))
    return [nums, threshold]
